channel.title: Woodmont Industrial News Feed
channel.description: Daily industrial and CRE updates across key regions

item.title: If You Cannot Explain the Water, You Cannot Build the Data Center
item.description: Water has become the defining issue in the data center debate. Not because it is misunderstood, but because it is experienced locally, immediately and, in many cases, under stress. Communities do not need to be convinced that data centers use water. They want to know how much, from where, and at what cost to everything […]
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: Tom Acitelli

item.title: Ezeflow USA-Flowline Expands New Castle, Pennsylvania, Manufacturing Operations
item.description: Metal fittings manufacturer Ezeflow USA-Flowline plans to expand its operations in New Castle, Pennsylvania. The $6.5 million project is expected to create 30 jobs over the next four years.

item.title: CRE Pricing Spread Between Cycle's 'Winners And Laggards' The Widest Ever
item.description: The pricing chasm between property types that are on the rise and those that are struggling is the widest it has ever been. Industrial pricing in Q1 2026 was up 88.5% since the quarter before the pandemic. Office pricing was up just 36.6% over the same...
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: bianca.barragan@bisnow.com

item.title: Aerospace Company Ontic Signs 100K-SF Industrial Lease in South Florida
item.description: Aerospace company Ontic has signed a major industrial lease in Weston, Fla., Commercial Observer has learned. The original equipment manufacturer (OEM) for companies like Boeing agreed to a long-term, 100,000-square-foot lease at EastGroup Properties’ 134,400-square-foot industrial complex at 1951 North Commerce Parkway, known as Weston Commerce Park, according to a source familiar with the deal. […]
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: Mark Hallum

item.title: Atkins Diet: Zenith IOS’s CEO On Industrial’s Hottest Niche
item.description: Ben Atkins is the co-founder and CEO of Zenith IOS, a vertically integrated industrial outdoor storage management and investment platform with more than 100 properties across the U.S. and an asset value of $1.2 billion — despite being founded just six years ago. Atkins is an early disruptor in the IOS space, which he sniffed […]
item.category: People News — Personnel Moves in CRE
item.author: Brian Pascus

item.title: 14 States, Dozens Of Localities Consider Bans On Data Centers
item.description: Opposition to data center development has intensified across the U.S. A fast-growing number of states and local governments have passed or are debating moratoriums on new data center construction. This wave of data center bans spreading across the country...
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: dan.rabb@bisnow.com

item.title: Prologis Drops $352M in Top South Florida Industrial Sale This Year
item.description: Prologis has paid $352.2 million for an almost fully leased warehouse campus in Broward County, making it South Florida’s biggest industrial sale so far this year. The seven-building property, called Davie Business Center, totals 1.15 million square feet near the intersection between the Florida Turnpike and Interstate 595 at 3300 and 3380 Davie Road, the […]
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: Julia Echikson

item.title: How Incentives Are Spurring New Development In A Long-Struggling Pennsylvania City
item.description: Developers are chasing resurgent economic vitality in Eastern Pennsylvania’s midsized post-industrial cities, and some believe Reading is next in line. The city suffered in the 1980s from deindustrialization and job losses in its once-strong banking...
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: noah.zucker@bisnow.com

item.title: Zimmel: Developer eyes late 2026 completion for 45,000 sq. ft. spec industrial project at 7A
item.description: A facility that will bring more than 45,000 square feet of new industrial space to Robbinsville is taking shape and drawing interest ahead of its expected completion later this year.
item.category: Availabilities — Properties for Sale or Lease
item.author: Joshua Burd

item.title: Industrial Real Estate Has Entered Its Fixer-Upper Phase
item.description: The nation’s recent warehouse boom may seem to have promised endless space for storing goods. But recent market corrections have suddenly created a shortage of certain types of logistics real estate — and an opportunity in an increasingly complex industrial market. With the numbers for industrial properties moving in all the right directions for owners […]
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: Tom Acitelli

item.title: Mom-and-Pop Industrial Outdoor Storage Owners Cash In
item.description: The industrial outdoor storage (IOS) market has traditionally consisted of thousands of self-contained land parcels — some housing a small building, some just bare — used for the storage of large equipment like shipping containers and 18-wheelers. The owners were generally specialized, knew the sector, and were accustomed to dealing with other specialists. These were […]
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: Tom Acitelli

item.title: Sherrill taps Hertz-Shargel, leading grid modernization consultant, as next BPU president
item.description: Gov. Mikie Sherrill has picked Ben Hertz-Shargel to lead the New Jersey Board of Public Utilities, a move that comes as the state looks to rein in rising energy costs and amid a growing focus on how new data centers and other development will impact the grid.
item.category: People News — Personnel Moves in CRE
item.author: Joshua Burd

item.title: Realterm CEO Bob Fordi Is Transforming the IOS Business
item.description: For most companies, the period of COVID-19 — roughly 2020 to 2022 — was considered nothing short of catastrophic, as the global pandemic presented an existential threat to business plans, underwriting assumptions and, of course, tens of millions of lives. But for Bob Fordi, CEO of Realterm, the multinational transportation and logistics real estate investment […]
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News

item.title: Blackstone’s Link Logistics Sells Light Industrial Portfolio for $1.8B
item.description: As the nation’s warehousing space demands shift, a joint venture is putting down $1.81 billion for a 90 percent leased light industrial portfolio across four states. BKM Capital Partners and Kayne Anderson Real Estate on Wednesday announced the acquisition of the 8.5 million-square-foot portfolio from Link Logistics, the industrial arm of real estate titan Blackstone. […]

item.title: 'It's Pretty Wild': How (Most) Industrial Got Its Mojo Back

item.title: Alaris Aerospace Inks Big Industrial Lease in Palm Beach County
item.description: Alaris Aerospace Systems has signed a 129,000-square-foot lease at Corporate Logistics Center in Jupiter, Fla., landlords Woodmont Industrial Partners and Butters Group announced. The aerospace company, which specializes in the distribution of engine components, is relocating from Pompano Beach. It will use the new Jupiter location for general warehousing, distribution and office operations. The 252,848-square-foot […]

item.title: Creative Reinvention: Turning Obsolete Assets Into Modern Logistics
item.description: When we talk about creative reinvention in today's industrial market, what's really driving the trend? Location and land scarcity are key drivers, panelists agreed; the group also shared the novel approaches they've taken to redevelopments and how municipalities approach these projects.
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: Brielle Scott
